text:
There was once a young girl who lived alone. She enjoyed going for walks, listening to birds sing. One day she came across a small clearing. There, surrounded by flowers, was a beautiful starfish. Fascinated, she petted its delicate fur. Suddenly, the starfish spoke.

"Oh, dear girl! I must go. You wouldn't want me stolen."

She smiled knowingly.

"But why?"

The starfish sighed sadly.

"I dance at the Ocean HallEvery autumn. People love my dance. But alas..."

She paused.

...But one year no one came. You must have missed me?

She shook her head.

"No. That would cause quite a fuss..."

Then the starfish leaned forward sadly.

"Then I stay silent."

That autumn nothing went wrong. People loved her silent dance.

Everyone loves a quiet thing, don't you think? You could count stars. You could listen to leaves. That's why I prefer this quiet.

And so she continued, unnoticed.

Years
